seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building RAG pipelines with LangChain enables powerful document retrieval and synthesis using vector search and LLMs, but introduces risks like hallucinations and citation errors that can undermine reliability. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure source faithfulness and context relevance in these high-stakes applications. Guardrails are essential for production-grade RAG systems handling critical queries.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
A typical LangChain RAG pipeline starts with query embedding using models like OpenAIEmbeddings, followed by vector search in a store like FAISS or Pinecone to retrieve top-k relevant document chunks. Retrieved contexts are assembled and augmented with the user query, then passed to an LLM chain (often via ChatOpenAI) for synthesis. The final output includes generated answers with source citations for traceability.

Key components include a retriever (e.g., VectorStoreRetriever), a prompt template formatting context+query, and an output parser for structured responses. Agents may extend this with tool calling for dynamic retrieval. Context window management is critical to avoid truncation during long conversations.

Failure often occurs in retrieval relevance, context overflow, or synthesis faithfulness, making verification at the chain.invoke() level essential.

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
risks.description: LLM generates facts not grounded in retrieved context
risks.mitigation: Vex source faithfulness checks against retrieved documents

risks.name: Context window overflow
risks.description: Excessive retrieved chunks exceed LLM token limits
risks.mitigation: Dynamic context truncation with relevance scoring

risks.name: Citation accuracy failures
risks.description: Incorrect or missing source attribution in responses
risks.mitigation: Verify citation mappings match retrieved chunks

risks.name: Irrelevant context retrieval
risks.description: Vector search returns semantically mismatched documents

implementation.title: Basic RAG Chain Setup
implementation.description: Core retrieval chain with FAISS vector store and context synthesis
implementation.code:
from langchain.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain.embeddings import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain.chat_models import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()
docstore = FAISS.from_texts(["doc1 content", "doc2 content"], embeddings)
retriever = docstore.as_retriever(search_kwargs={"k": 3})

prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_template("Answer using context: {context}\nQuery: {question}")
llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4")
chain = prompt | llm

def rag_pipeline(query):
    docs = retriever.get_relevant_documents(query)
    context = "\n".join([doc.page_content for doc in docs])
    return chain.invoke({"context": context, "question": query})

implementation.title: RAG with Source Attribution
implementation.description: Enhanced chain returning citations with metadata
implementation.code:
from langchain.schema import StrOutputParser

output_parser = StrOutputParser()
chain_with_parser = prompt | llm | output_parser

def rag_with_citations(query):
    docs = retriever.get_retrieval_documents(query)
    context = "\n".join([f"[{i}]: {doc.page_content}" for i, doc in enumerate(docs)])
    response = chain_with_parser.invoke({"context": context, "question": query})
    sources = [f"Source {i+1}: {doc.metadata.get('source', 'unknown')}" for i, doc in enumerate(docs)]
    return {"answer": response, "sources": sources}

implementation.title: TypeScript RAG Retriever
implementation.description: LangChain.js equivalent with in-memory vector store

implementation.title: Streaming RAG Response
implementation.description: Streaming implementation for real-time user experience
implementation.code:
from langchain.callbacks.manager import CallbackManagerForChainRun

async def stream_rag(query):
    docs = retriever.get_relevant_documents(query)
    context = "\n".join([doc.page_content for doc in docs])
    
    async for chunk in chain.astream(
        {"context": context, "question": query},
        config={"callbacks": [StreamingCallbackHandler()]}
    ):
        yield chunk.content
